title: ED syncope documentation

presenting_history.text: Supports recording what occurred before, during and after the event, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, injury, duration, confusion and focal weakness from patient and witness accounts.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact event-history section supports onset, event duration, severity or injury impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and patient or witness concerns for suspected transient loss of consciousness without diagnosing syncope.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports recording what occurred before, during and after the event, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, injury, duration, confusion and focal weakness from patient and witness accounts.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-recorded urgent-risk context including ECG abnormalities, heart failure signs, exertional events, family history, unexplained breathlessness and murmur without generating urgency or referral.
red_flags.rationale: The exact urgent-feature section supports recording time-critical findings, clinician-decided disposition and completed specialist discussion; it does not generate an urgency conclusion.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-recorded urgent-risk context including ECG abnormalities, heart failure signs, exertional events, family history, unexplained breathlessness and murmur without generating urgency or referral.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

examination.text: Supports prior-event, medical, family and medicine history, measured vital signs, cardiovascular and neurological signs, ECG status and clinician-selected testing when an underlying problem is suspected.
examination.rationale: The exact initial-assessment section supports clinician risk context, measured vital signs, focused cardiovascular or neurological examination, severity, appearance and existing ECG or test results only when assessed or reviewed.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports prior-event, medical, family and medicine history, measured vital signs, cardiovascular and neurological signs, ECG status and clinician-selected testing when an underlying problem is suspected.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.2.1–1.1.2.4 — history, examination, vital signs, ECG and selected tests

treatment_and_management.text: Supports careful recording of all accounts and transfer of paramedic, ECG and patient-report records to the receiving clinician.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact record-transfer section supports recording existing ECG or report transfer, clinician documentation plan and arranged handover without selecting a destination or diagnosis.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports careful recording of all accounts and transfer of paramedic, ECG and patient-report records to the receiving clinician.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.1.3.1 — recording and transfer of records

source_limitations: Exact event circumstances, witness account, posture, prodrome, movements, recovery, family history, medication context, orthostatic measurements, ECG findings, diagnosis, risk score and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
